name: parley-vscode
description: Interact with MIT Parley agents from Visual Studio Code with safe context sharing and diff review.

parley.endpoint.markdownDescription: Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible MIT Parley API.

parley.defaultAgent.markdownDescription: Default model identifier (e.g. `bedrock/claude-sonnet-4-6`, `openai/gpt-5.1`, `google/gemini-3.1-pro`). Run `Parley: Set API Key` then pick from the model dropdown in the chat view.

parley.stream.markdownDescription: Stream Parley replies token-by-token in the chat view.

enumDescriptions: Adaptive — the model decides how much to reason (required for Claude Opus 4.7 on Bedrock)
parley.thinking.markdownDescription: Extended thinking: makes the model reason step-by-step before answering, sent as the Parley `thinking` parameter. Supported on Claude, OpenAI reasoning models, and Gemini. Enabled levels also raise `max_tokens` to leave room for the answer. Thinking increases output-token usage (and cost). Also switchable from the **Mode** popover in the composer.

enumDescriptions: Answer only — no agent, no file access
Agent tools; approve each edit in a diff
Agent reads the repo read-only and proposes a plan
Agent decides and applies edits automatically
CAUTION: auto-applies edits AND runs shell commands without asking
parley.defaultMode.markdownDescription: Default chat **Mode**, also switchable from the Mode popover in the composer. Shell commands always require confirmation regardless of mode.

parley.autoContinue.markdownDescription: In agent modes, keep working automatically (up to a safety cap) until the task is complete, instead of stopping for you to say "continue". The agent signals completion with a `<DONE>` marker.

parley.maxToolRounds.markdownDescription: Maximum tool-call rounds the agent may take within a single turn.

parley.maxAutoContinue.markdownDescription: Maximum number of times Parley auto-continues a turn before pausing for you. `0` disables auto-continue.

parley.tokenLimit.markdownDescription: Token budget per conversation. When the conversation's total token usage reaches this, Parley stops and asks you to raise the limit or start a new conversation. **`0` means unlimited (default).** Set it from `Parley: Set Token Limit`.

parley.autoCompactTokens.markdownDescription: When the conversation's estimated size exceeds this many tokens, automatically compact (summarize) it before the next turn to control cost and avoid context-window errors. **`0` disables (default).** (Compaction is lossy — it replaces history with a summary.) The agent loop also trims stale tool outputs automatically regardless of this setting.

parley.inlineCompletion.enabled.markdownDescription: Show Parley inline (ghost-text) code completions as you type. Toggle quickly with `Parley: Toggle Inline Completion`.

parley.inlineCompletion.model.markdownDescription: Model used for inline completions. Prefer a fast, inexpensive model.

parley.inlineCompletion.debounceMs.markdownDescription: Idle delay before requesting an inline completion, in milliseconds.

parley.context.maxCharacters.markdownDescription: Maximum characters of code/context to include in a request.

parley.context.includeDiagnostics.markdownDescription: Include VS Code diagnostics when commands request problem context.

parley.context.respectGitignore.markdownDescription: Respect workspace .gitignore patterns when collecting file context.

parley.confirmBeforeSendingLargeContext.markdownDescription: Show a context preview and confirmation before sending larger requests.

parley.telemetry.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable extension telemetry. This extension currently sends no telemetry.

parley.logLevel.markdownDescription: Controls extension output verbosity. Secrets and prompt contents are never logged.
